text: Assistance for passengers of reduced mobility Passengers with reduced mobility can use our travel assistance service, MyWay. It includes mobility aids, as well as skilled professionals who will provide you with assistance according to your needs during your passage through the airport.

section: Who can use the MyWay service?
text: The MyWay service is available for any passenger with disability or reduced mobility departing, in transit or arriving at the airport.

section: How do I book the MyWay service?
text: You must request this service from your airline or travel agent upon booking your trip, or up to 48 hours before the published time for the departure of the flight. The information will be transmitted by the airline to the relevant airports, to ensure all the assistance is provided.

section: Can I be accompanied by my family or group during special assistance?
text: If you have booked assistance and are unable to travel through the airport without a carer or companion, we will ensure that one of them can accompany you throughout the entire journey. If you are travelling in a larger group then it would be best to nominate one person to stay with you. Companions may be asked to vacate our mobility equipment should it be required for special assistance passengers.

section: What kind of assistance is provided by MyWay?
text: MyWay assistance includes a range of services and equipment to make your journey through the airport more accessible. This includes escalators, lifts, moving walkways, clear signage and guidance, as well as escort and support provided by specialized and duly identified professionals, during passenger embarking and disembarking.

section: What kind of assistance is provided when the trip begins at the Airport?
text: Upon arriving at the airport, look for the Designated Point of Arrival - a telephone booth clearly marked with the MyWay logo. Using the phone, inform the MyWay service of your arrival, for personal and baggage assistance services at check-in, security checkpoint, border and boarding, all the way to your seat on the aircraft, where the airport's responsibility ends.

section: And what kind of assistance can I get when arriving or waiting for a connecting flight at the airport?
text: When disembarking, the airline or travel agent will notify the MyWay service. All the necessary personal and baggage assistance shall be provided from your seat on the aircraft to the next point of departure.

section: Is the MyWay service properly identified at the airport?
text: At the airport, you will find signage directing you to the MyWay service contact points, which identify all and any resources or mechanisms available for people with reduced mobility.

text: (click on the images to zoom in)

section: Are there special parking spaces for people with reduced mobility?
text: Private vehicles bearing the reduced mobility sign have parking available in all of the airport's parks. The designated parking spaces, located near the exits are properly identified.

section: Are guide dogs allowed into the airport?
text: Guide dogs are allowed to circulate around the airport, including in boarding terminals. However, you should contact your airline or travel agent to confirm that guide dogs are allowed on the aircraft.Learn more about traveling with animals.

section: Are passengers with reduced mobility also protected?
text: Yes, passengers with disabilities or reduced mobility are protected against discrimination and, under certain conditions, benefit from the appropriate assistance at all EU airports. See our page on passenger rights to learn more. Alameda das Comunidades Portuguesas
